task_completion.description: Agent initiates segmentation or thresholding on the loaded CT dataset.
task_completion.visual_evidence: InVesalius GUI shows active masking, thresholding, or segmentation tools being applied to the 2D DICOM slices (axial, coronal, or sagittal views), typically highlighting bone structures.

task_completion.description: Agent isolates the mandible (lower jaw) from the rest of the skull.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The mask or 3D view shows ONLY the lower jaw. The upper jaw (maxilla) and the rest of the cranium should be erased, cropped, or unselected using manual editing, region growing, or cutting tools.

task_completion.description: Agent generates a 3D surface of the isolated mandible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The bottom-right 3D viewer pane in InVesalius displays a 3D rendered surface consisting solely of the mandible (lower jaw bone), without the rest of the skull.

task_completion.description: Agent accesses the export 3D surface functionality.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An 'Export 3D surface' dialog box is open, or the File -> Export menu is visibly being navigated.

task_completion.description: Agent saves the isolated mandible as a binary STL file to the correct path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A save file dialog is visible showing the directory '/home/ga/Documents/' and the filename 'mandible.stl' with the STL file format selected.

integrity.description: Agent used the InVesalius 3 GUI to perform the segmentation and export.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show interaction with the InVesalius interface (clicking tools, adjusting thresholds, viewing 3D models) rather than executing a Python script or terminal command to bypass the UI.

integrity.description: The exported model is genuinely an isolated mandible, not the entire skull.
integrity.visual_evidence: The 3D surface visible in the viewer or export preview clearly lacks the upper skull, eye sockets, and nasal cavity, showing only the U-shaped lower jaw.

integrity.description: Agent did not use terminal commands to fake the output file.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no terminal windows showing commands like 'touch /home/ga/Documents/mandible.stl' or copying an existing file to the target location.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The target output path is exactly '/home/ga/Documents/mandible.stl'. The task requires isolating the mandible (lower jaw) from the rest of the skull. A full skull has a vertical height of ~180-220mm, whereas an isolated mandible is much shorter (~65-67mm). The VLM must strictly verify that the 3D surface generated and exported visually represents ONLY the lower jaw, with the maxilla (upper jaw) and cranium completely removed.
